reasoning: The disassembly contains multiple functions; the clearest high-level control flow is the command-processing loop starting near 0x15c0.

A loop repeatedly prints a prompt, reads input, tokenizes it, checks the first token against three built-in commands, dispatches either to a built-in handler or an external-command executor, then frees memory.
Branches arise from command comparisons, empty-input handling, dispatch selection, and the final continuation test that either loops back or exits.
